programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: After a faulty interview with the Life Foundation ruins his career, former reporter Eddie Brock's life is in pieces. Six months later, he comes across the Life Foundation again, and he comes into contact with an alien symbiote and becomes Venom, a parasitic antihero.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
programme.desc: John Wick is forced out of retirement by a former associate looking to seize control of a shadowy international assassins’ guild. Bound by a blood oath to aid him, Wick travels to Rome and does battle against some of the world’s most dangerous killers.

programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: After success cleaning up Dodge City, Wyatt Earp moves to Tombstone, Arizona, and wishes to get rich in obscurity. He meets his brothers there, as well as his old friend Doc Holliday. A band of outlaws that call themselves The Cowboys are causing problems in the region with various acts of random violence, and inevitably come into confrontation with Holliday and the Earps, which leads to a shoot-out at the O.K. Corral.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Raiders of the Lost Ark
programme.desc: When Dr. Indiana Jones – the tweed-suited professor who just happens to be a celebrated archaeologist – is hired by the government to locate the legendary Ark of the Covenant, he finds himself up against the entire Nazi regime.

programme.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
programme.desc: An apocalyptic story set in the furthest reaches of our planet, in a stark desert landscape where humanity is broken, and most everyone is crazed fighting for the necessities of life. Within this world exist two rebels on the run who just might be able to restore order.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection
programme.desc: Arthur Bishop thought he had put his murderous past behind him when his most formidable foe kidnaps the love of his life. Now he is forced to travel the globe to complete three impossible assassinations, and do what he does best, make them look like accidents.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: An art collector appeals to Indiana Jones to embark on a search for the Holy Grail. He learns that another archaeologist has disappeared while searching for the precious goblet, and the missing man is his own father, Dr. Henry Jones. The artifact is much harder to find than they expected, and its powers are too much for those impure of heart.

programme.title: Baby Driver
programme.desc: Coerced into working for a crime boss, a young getaway driver must face the music when a doomed heist threatens his life, love, and freedom.

programme.title: Constantine
programme.desc: John Constantine has literally been to Hell and back. When he teams up with a policewoman to solve the mysterious suicide of her twin sister, their investigation takes them through the world of demons and angels that exists beneath the landscape of contemporary Los Angeles.

programme.title: The Punisher
programme.desc: After Frank Castle's whole family is gunned down in cold blood, he shall become the vengeful Angel of Death known as the Great Equaliser of Crime: The Punisher. The one-man hurricane of blood and bullets is about to make the thugs who destroyed his life rue the day they made The Punisher come to life.

programme.title: Universal Soldier
programme.desc: An elite team of soldiers has been used against terrorists where they use astounding physical capabilities to overcome them. Victoria, a reporter, follows them and discovers a part of their secret. When one of the team kills her cameraman she tries to escape. Luc, one of the soldiers begins to have flashbacks and turns sides and helps her as the remainder of the team follows to protect their secrets.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
Oingo Boingo Live At The Ritz (1985)
John Lennon and the plastic ono band Toronto 1969
Van Halen - Live Without a Net (1986)
Columbo
programme.desc: The mistress of a conductor becomes a target for murder after threatening to tell all to her lover's wife.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude feels her life is in danger from an oversexed housepainter, and reacts hysterically.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude heads for the bottle when she sees an old friend in a wheelchair.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche's sister, Virginia, comes to Miami for a visit and Blanche is convinced she's after something. Later, while out to dinner, she learns just what Virginia is after, Blanche's kidney. Meanwhile, Rose and Dorothy babysit for an infant.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: A doctor Dorothy dates makes a pass at Blanche. But when Blanche tells Dorothy about it, Dorothy doesn't believe her. Dorothy accuses her of being a slut and when Blanche can't take no more she orders Dorothy to move out.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche's 14-year old grandson comes to Miami for a visit and brings a new meaning to the term "generation gap." Meanwhile, Dorothy studies for her French final.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: A dead body, a news crew and a health inspector all descend upon the hotel during its weekend-long re-opening celebrations leaving the staff scrambling.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: While collecting junk, Lamont runs across an old suitcase and decides it could be worth something. However, once he gets it home, Fred makes a shocking discovery when he opens the case to discover that it is stuffed with money. Lamont wants to turn the money in and Fred wants to keep it and locks in a safe. Just then a gunman shows up wanting the money.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Edith is reunited with her childhood sweetheart when she returns to her hometown for a wedding.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: A contest between Archie and Mike, to see who can abstain from his favorite indulgence (Archie's being smoking cigars, Mike's eating everything in sight) becomes a battle of wills. Who will win this 48-hour contest?

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike accepts a job, which pleases Archie because it means that Mike and Gloria will be moving. Finding a new home proves difficult. George Jefferson schemes to rent his old house next door to them cheap, just to annoy Archie.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Marty & Tracie vs. Jeannie & Dena vs. Robin & Amy

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Kathy & Sharry vs. Robin & Von vs. Ann & Joe

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges witness an armed robbery and are brought in by the cops as suspects. After passing a lie detector test, the boys are freed but are now the only ones who can identify the crooks. Meanwhile, their friend Gladys has inherited a house in the country and the boys go with her to inspect it so she won't be gypped when its sold. The house turns out to be the crook's hideout, and when they abduct Gladys, the stooges must rescue her.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc:
The trio are employed as handymen in a recording studio. While not doing their work, they hear a recording session as a woman sings "Voices of Spring." Impressed by the operatic virtuosity of this stunningly beautiful soprano, Curly lip syncs, as the other stooges dress him as a woman.
Curly (in drag) is "heard" by the radio host. Moe dubs Curly "Señorita Cucaracha," and the trio are hired to sing professionally on the radio, but must also appear at the home of the radio show’s sponsor for a party.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are royal guards against an evil prince.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges run a tailor shop and need money to pay their creditors. A bank robber leaves his coat in the shop with a combination to a safe. When the crook comes back to retrieve the coat, the stooges capture him and get his bankroll.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are fix-it men who are brought before a judge on a charge of chicken stealing. They escape from the courtroom and wind up getting hired in the judges' house after their antics attempting to fix the doorbell cause the servants to quit. The boys are discovered when the cake they bakes explodes all over a political supporter of the judge and he loses his chance for re-election

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are frontier guides leading a minstrel show west. When hostile Indians run the horses run off they are stranded. They must contend with a snow storm and a marauding bear as well the Indians. After almost killing each other ice fishing they solve their problems by rigging up a sail on the wagon and sailing west. A wagon train full of show biz girls tours the West, and the Stooges are assigned to protect them from Indians. First appearance by Stooge player Dorothy Appleby (former Miss Maine).

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are tree surgeons who are enlisted by a rich old man to find a mate for his rare puckerless persimmon tree. The boys sail to the tropical island of Rhum-Boogie to find the tree. When they arrive they are captured by the natives and will be eaten unless Curly marries the Chief's ugly daughter. The stooges escape with the tree and, after a confrontation with an alligator, sail off with their prize.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The new wife next door, Kathy Bowman, looks down on Roseanne and complains that D.J. is a bad influence on her son Todd.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne helps Crystal with her Lamaze classes; Dan's near-fatal accident at work makes D.J. follow him obsessively; Dan and Roseanne sit up all night doing their wills, and Jackie is offended that she doesn't get custody of the kids.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne tries to convince Darlene to wear something feminine to a big school dance -- but she'd rather attract attention for her softball skills.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The family barbecue sees Nana Mary's first appearance on the show. Family and friends (except Dan's father and Roseanne's mother) gather at the Conner household for food, drink and singing, and Roseanne's fellow waitress Bonnie, gets a chance to show what a great singer she really is. Beverley still manages to upset Roseanne and Jackie from the other end of the phone, but Nana Mary turns the tables on her.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan and Roseanne commit to opening a motorcycle shop with a free-spirited old friend who gets cold feet at the last minute.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is stunned when Becky asks for advice about birth control.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan open up shop at Lanford Custom Cycle, but selling a bike proves an exhausting challenge, as does keeping the household fine-tuned with Becky in charge.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie awakens and is horrified to find that she has slept with Arnie; Dan invites Leon home for the weekly poker game; D.J. trades his comics for a dog.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: If appearances are to be believed, Captain Peacock may have had an affair with Mr. Rumbold's pretty young secretary - an engaged secretary, no less - but do people have the wrong idea about what happened?

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill and Toony get a visit from everyone's favorite robot, Me-Man.
Featured cartoons: The Heckling Hare (1941) Les Miserobots (1968) Pink Outs (1967) The Spinach Overture (1935) Tired and Feathered (1965)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: A surgeon has an ingenious plan for murdering his partner in a research project, but a paranoid nurse quickly catches onto his scheme, so he kills her. Lt. Columbo has a tough time figuring this one out.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline show their true colors when the diner gets robbed. Meanwhile, everyone sees a surprising new side of Han.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: On this edition of Prime Time Wrestling, Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host an exciting lineup of in-ring competition, featuring Rick Martel and King Haku meeting one-on-one. Plus, Ted DiBiase finally receives a championship belt worthy of his name, The Red Rooster takes on Mr. Perfect, and more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: A bank robber holds up a bank across the street from Bob’s Burgers. When the robbery goes awry, Bob finds himself at the center of a hostage crisis.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray feel tremendous pressure to show each other a good time on a romantic weekend getaway in Vermont. From the moment they set foot in the quaint bed and breakfast, it’s apparent that Debra and Ray are trying a bit too hard for this to be a perfect weekend.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra gets a part-time job at an AD agency, an unhappy Ray is left to fend for himself and Marie is left wondering what’s to become of the children, the house, and her beloved son now that they will be neglected.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug's bowling team bowls just for fun and is financially supported by the local ale house, but now they threaten to no longer support them if they continue to be on a constant losing streak. Doug soon realizes Carrie is the problem so he removes her from the team making for an awkward household.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie wants to invest Doug's Christmas bonus; Arthur competes with the neighbors about who has the largest yard decoration.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George objects to his girlfriend's roommate; Super Bowl tickets make the rounds as plans change.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George fails to keep a secret about a friend; Kramer fingers Newman as a scofflaw.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Foreman Dan feels lower than low for getting tough with his employees at work, while Roseanne lectures David for getting high after she finds a stash of pot in the house -- then realizes it is hers from decades ago.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After months of trying to get pregnant, Roseanne learns that Jackie is expecting -- and Fred is the unknowing father.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael was excited to know Dunder Mifflin bought television ad space in local markets, including Scranton. Michael wanted to create the perfect commercial. Only problem was Michael wasn't seeing eye to eye with guys who were going to be producing the commercial.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Karen was now the regional manager of the Utica branch and used her position of power to woo Stanley to her branch. For the right price, Stanley agreed to make the transfer. In Scranton the news was welcomed with applause, much to Michael's dismay.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A new TV series features a suave and debonair character named Homer Simpson. Homer capitalizes on his new namesake, until the character is rewritten to be a bumbling fool. Trying to recapture the positive attention he enjoyed, Homer changes his name to "Max Power."

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Apu is making the husbands of Springfield look bad with his extravagent Valentine's Day efforts for Manjula. When the men try to sabotage Apu's grand gesture, they accidentally wind up benefiting from it.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer buys a Canyonero utility vehicle but finds it was intended for women to drive so he gives it to Marge. Marge soon gets into her new vehicle,becoming prone to road rage,for which she arrested by Wiggum. However,when the rest of the family are trapped by an escaped rhinoceros at the zoo,Marge's reckless driving comes in handy at saving them.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Indebted from a mishap at the Smithsonian, Homer lets a cell phone company put a transmitter in the house. Forced to share a room with Bart, Lisa resents Homer until a New Age experience gives her empathy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After beating Homer in a steak-eating competition, a trucker dies; Homer and Bart take over his rig to complete his shipment.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al is excited when he's asked to speak at the Polk High Homecoming Dance. At the dance, while Al rants on and on about the unhappily married man, Peggy finds herself being wooed by a teacher, Kelly is trying to please two dates and Bud is plotting revenge against his date, Heather McCoy, who humiliated him years earlier by running his underwear up a flagpole.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al plans a special evening for Peggy's birthday by taking her out to the Bowlarama where he hopes to finally beat his arch rival's high game score. Al beats the score, but minutes later, Peggy bowls a perfect game, destroying Al's self-esteem.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy learns that she never got her high school diploma because she failed home economics during her senior year. Using her "life experience" as course credit, she takes the final exam with Kelly's class to get her diploma.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When Kelly realizes that she's not intelligent or literate enough to attend college, she takes a job at a TV station, where she quickly moves from intern to a $1,000-a-week weather bunny. The current weatherman storms off and Kelly, with her new manager, Al, gets a $250,000-a-year contract offer.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After being plagued by nightmares of feet, a beautiful woman asks Al to judge a contest at Gary's Shoes, whose contestants she "could never compete with". Meanwhile, Kelly tricks Marcy and Bud into thinking they slept together.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After discovering Peggy's latest purchases, Al throws a yard sale to get rid of a lot of the junk, but no one is interested.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: On Labor Day weekend, with Kelly having graduated from high school, Peggy still not working, Bud in his senior year in high school and Marcy officially divorced and having gotten a promotion at her bank, Al decides to take the family for a vacation and 'see America' by car in their red Mustang convertible. But they end up being caught in a 50-mile traffic jam on the Chicago freeway the entire weekend.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred's friend, Yerby is the manager of a champion boxer, Junior Joe Louis. Fred goes in and buys half ownership. Fred then begins training the boxer for an upcoming fight but soon runs into trouble. For Junior is pulled from the fight because of to Fred's stubbornness.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred cleans the house from top to bottom and Lamont is stunned. Then he learns the reason: Fred's baby sister, Frances is visiting to announce her engagement. Fred is excited, but he soon finds her fiance, Rodney is white.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont has had a chronic headache for three days and isn't sure what's causing it. A doctor suggests that Lamont consult a psychiatrist since the cause may be stress. Once at the psychiatrist's office, the doctor determines that Fred is the cause of Lamont's headaches.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont has Julio, Rollo and another buddy over for a poker game and Fred has had it with their gambling. Meanwhile, an old friend of Fred's, Al Banks, a professional gambler, stops by for a visit and this gives Fred an idea on how to teach Lamont and his friends a lesson. However, the lesson goes awry and Al runs out with the cash.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris loses his wallet in the big city, and the big city give him the key to the city and other perks to make him feel better.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: When Chris was 12 he got a job as a paperboy so he could buy a submarine from the back of a comic book. Four to six hundred weeks later when the kit arrives, Chris and his father put it together; however when they embark on the sub's maiden voyage (in Chris's bathtub) they become trapped.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Parody of famous gangster pictures.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Spot-gag spoof of the Believe It, or Not! franchise.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky falls asleep after refusing to say the Pledge of Allegiance. Uncle Sam comes to him in his dreams and explains to him what the pledge means, and how it honors those who gave their life for the nation. Porky sees the error of his ways.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Parody of The Shooting of Dan McGrew.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Travelogue parody of a look all-round America.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Travelogue parody of a cruise ship travelling to Alaska.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky the police officer must investigate a haunted house.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis and Butt-head vs. The Vending Machine
programme.desc:
Beavis and Butt-head try various means to get their bag of snacks unstuck and out of the vending machine before they starve. videos:
Sweaty Nipples - "Demon Juice"
Elton John & RuPaul - "Don't Go Breakin' My Heart"
Seaweed - "Kid Candy"
Motley Crue - "Houligan's Holiday"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head think their sperms are holding them back from scoring and decide to get vasectomies.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head miss the schoolbus and decide to take a shortcut home.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go to a trailer park during a tornado and (almost) get to ""do it"" with Lolita & Tanqueray.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: When Beavis finds a camera, the boys photograph their butts and their ""captains,"" and return the camera for a reward.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head are recruited by Stewart's mom to find her missing little man.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After Beavis is challenged to a fight, Butt-Head trains him to become a champion.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis & Butt-head find a dead squirrel and try to sell its fur.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head deal with annoying customers (and deep fry stuff) during a shift at Burger World.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys decide to get skull earrings -- piercing their own ears is the hard part.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head try wishing on a bottle hoping their is a genie inside... but the bottle they wish upon only has a dead mouse in it.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Van Driessen picks the boys to chaperone Japanese foreign exchange student Hiroshi ""Hiro"" Nakayama; they teach him how to hock loogies, play mudball (which causes Mr. Anderson to have a Bataan flashback)...and flog his dolphin.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.sub-title: Now You See Me, Now You Don't
programme.desc: Today's secret word is really "little," but the gangs' eyes get really big when Pee-wee puts on a magic show and disappears! Captain Carl, Cowboy Curtis, Miss Yvonne, and the gang are amazed as Pee-wee makes use of his new talent at their expense, but when their hidden host can't make himself reappear, it'll take Jambi's magic to set things right!

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Cowboy Curtis decides to have a cowboy-style snack (slow-cooked steak) and shows Pee-wee how to square dance. Pee-wee shows Curtis how to pogo, and Dixie gets her name on the "back" of her new jacket.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to take their cupcake business to the next level by auditioning for the reality cooking show Cupcake Wars.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: A disparaging remark about his wife sets Johnny Sack off on a vendetta. Meadow decides to do volunteer work at the South Bronx Law Center; Furio hosts a housewarming party for the gang; Silvio and Chris make an offer to an old-time hit man named Lou DiMaggio; and Tony and Carmela find themselves at odds over the family's bill-consolidation strategy.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Checking out Ralph's promising new filly at the track, Tony displays a flair for horse-racing strategy--and discovers an unexpected affinity for the stable life. Adriana finds her rock club being used as a place of business for Chris and his pals. Meanwhile, a court artist incurs the wrath of Uncle Junior, and Janice feeds Bobby in his hour of need.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor makes a surprising personal discovery. Meanwhile, Michael tasks an indecisive Chidi with coming to an important decision and Jianyu makes a big announcement.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor and Tahani hatch a plan while Michael makes a startling discovery. Meanwhile, Chidi confides in Eleanor after learning some interesting news.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As the families gather on Driftmark for a funeral, Viserys calls for an end to infighting and Alicent demands justice.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: In the wake of a costly victory, Jon and Daenerys look to the south as Tyrion eyes a compromise that could save countless lives.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: After recovering from his gunshot wounds, House works feverishly on two cases at the same time: a paralyzed man who drove his wheelchair into a swimming pool and a woman who became paralyzed after a yoga session.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House and the team treat a young boy who claims there is a tracking device in his neck and believes he has been the subject of alien experimentation. Cameron is outraged when she learns Cuddy and Wilson have been lying to House about the diagnosis on his last case.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Grampa invents an aphrodisiac tonic,which kick-starts the sex lives of most of the adults in Springfield and he and Homer go on the road to sell it. However at an old farm-house where the Simpsons once lived,Grampa reveals to Homer that his birth was accidental. Homer is angry and the two part. But after reflection,they reconcile back at the farm-house but also manage to burn it down.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons are about to go on vacation, but Marge panics on the airplane and demands to get off. After denying there's anything wrong to her family, then goes into therapy to find the root cause of her aerophobia.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer secretly trails Lenny and Carl to the venue of a masonic society called the Stonecutters,who enjoy elite privileges and seem to number every male in Springfield except him. When a birthmark on him shaped like the secret sign of the Stonecutters is revealed he is declared their new leader but,unhappy at the premise of having to obey him,the lodge found another society - called No Homers.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The story of Maggie's birth is told in flashbacks: after Homer quits the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant to pursue his dream of working at a bowling alley, Marge reveals that she is pregnant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart is forced to assist Principal Skinner in early-morning astronomy observations, he discovers a giant comet heading towards Springfield. Facing impending doom, the entire town shows up at the Flanders family bomb shelter.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty the Clown's reckless spending forces him to open a clown college to keep afloat. New graduate Homer discovers the perks and perils of being Krusty.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tells Bart that toilet water swirls in the opposite direction in the Southern Hemisphere. Determined to find out if this is true, Bart makes a collect call to Australia. When the father of the boy he called finds out about the charges, it sparks an international incident. The Simpsons must travel to Australia to repair relations with the United States.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A loan from Patty and Selma makes Homer their slave; Bart is forced to take ballet.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: It is decided that a film festival should be held to put Springfield on the map and cinema critic Jay Sherman comes to town and stays with the Simpsons. Various people submit films that they have made to win the critics' prize, from Burns' blockbuster presenting him as an epic hero to Barney's strangely poetic monochrome short, but the most popular choice seems to be George C. Scott in 'Man Getting Hit With a Football'.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A fortuneteller predicts Lisa's engagement and wedding to a fellow student from an upper-crust British family.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: Mr. McMahon demands to know if The Undertaker has an alliance with Kane. WWE Champion "Stone Cold" Steve Austin faces Mankind & Kane in a Handicap Match. Triple H defends his European Title against D'lo Brown. Plus, Mr. Yamaguchi-San punishes his wife for her affair with Val Venis and X-Pac challenges The Rock for his Intercontinental Championship.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Looking for privacy, Johnny and Moira take the Mayor up on his offer of using his cabin for a night, while Alexis and David use the opportunity to throw a party.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is hired to be a local winery's spokesperson; David struggles with a health problem.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline discovers the secret ingredient to Max's cupcakes whilst in the midst of her new obsession couponing.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's new neighbor draws their suspicion.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to prove their cleaning skills to Sophie so she will hire them for her maid service.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie's (Jennifer Coolidge) beauty overwhelms Earl, Caroline and Max wind up spending Valentine's Day at the hospital; Caroline tries to rekindle an old flame.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline have to make kosher cupcakes when they get a gig at a bar mitzvah.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max runs into an old boyfriend, when she accompanies Caroline to see her father in jail.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend their spring break dog-sitting at a couple's lavish apartment.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max convinces Caroline to partake in a clinical drug trial in an effort to raise money to help Caroline’s father.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After promising Earl to drop his tax return in the mail, Caroline learns that Max has never filed, and gets sidetracked trying to help her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The stakes are high when a socialite Manhattan mother hires Max and Caroline to serve cupcakes at her daughter's first birthday party.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And Martha Stewart Have a Ball (1)
programme.desc: Max and Caroline plan to attend a big fashion gala at the Metropolitan Museum of Art where Martha Stewart is scheduled to be a guest, in an attempt to have her taste their cupcakes and get her seal of approval.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to keep faith that Martha Stewart, who tasted and liked one of their cupcakes in last season's finale, will call and help support their business. Meanwhile, Caroline loses a lucky keepsake.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline show their true colors when the diner gets robbed. Meanwhile, everyone sees a surprising new side of Han.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to take their cupcake business to the next level by auditioning for the reality cooking show Cupcake Wars.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: With his wife and son visiting from America, Ted makes drastic changes to the lineup during a critical match.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: When Jamie refuses to train, Ted turns to talented new signing Dani Rojas—and the team is struck by an age-old curse.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Rebecca celebrates her first anniversary as a divorcée as the team plays a match in Liverpool.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals how microwave ovens work and shows a cross-section model of a spider that demonstrates how they spin silk. He als discusses how they can be a crucial part of our ecosystem by eating harmful insects.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the human ear, why we have earwax, and why they pop when they are under pressure. He also introduces Robert Goddard, the father of modern rocketry.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses the ozone layer and why it is rapidly depleting. He also explains the complexities of acids.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses how plumbing works, and why the world is a better place with it. He also explains how and why roller coasters work.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discusses how bees live and produce honey. He also explains how shifting plates on the earth's surface cause earthquakes.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Don't stay in the dark - Bill Nye will help you absorb the science of light optics. Light is energy that normally moves in a straight line, but often something gets in the way. When light runs into something, three things can happen - the light can bounce off, it can go through, or it can be absorbed. Often all three things happen at the same time. Light bounces off mirrors. You see yourself in a mirror when light bounces off your face, into the mirror, and then into your eyes. Light goes through glass. If the glass is bent or curved, the light gets bent on its way out of the glass. The glass in a magnifying glass or a pair of eyeglasses is curved so that it bends light, making things look bigger. More light is absorbed by dark-colored things than by light-colored things. Colors are made when some light is absorbed while other light is bounced back. Black things look black because when light hits them, they absorb almost all of the light.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: In this show, you can Bone up on Muscles. When you clicked on the Nye Labs web site to read this, you used your bones and muscles. Without them, you can't click, surf, or even sigh. Bones and muscles work together, or you aren't going anywhere. Muscles always pull, even when you push on something like a door somewhere in your body your arm and leg muscles are in tension. They are all attached to bones, and those bones are pushing; they're in compression. By pulling on bones you can breathe, talk, and move all over the world. Your bones support your weight like beams of steel or wood. They're stiff and strong. Rigid as they might seem though, they do flex. And, if you bang one hard enough, it swells up. You have a lump. That's because bones are full of blood vessels. Bones are not solid like rocks or skeletons in a dinosaur museum. Bones flex and grow. In fact, putting healthy amounts of stress on your bones is good for them.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball is asked to settle a dispute between Penny and her family.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin find themselves in serious trouble when they agree to everything the new kid in school says. The truth is they can't understand a word that he says. Peter Pepperoni has been home schooled and developed a very odd way of speaking. Due to a series of misunderstandings Gumball and Darwin end up on the run from the law.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: To make ends meet, the Wattersons are forced to rent out their house. However, with nowhere else to stay they decide to secretly remain at the house. When they accidentally surprise their guests, an elderly pair of fainting goats, they faint...unsurprisingly. The Wattersons fear the worst and attempt to cover up this "accident".

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Halloween isn't what it used to be. Can Gumball and Darwin help the ghouls get their scare back?

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Mr. Small goes feral after Gumball and Darwin expose his hypocrisy.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball decides to engross himself in plant culture to appease Leslie after making an offensive comment.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard misses a package and is forced into an escalating feud with the delivery guy.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball tries to protect Darwin from his unsuitable undead girlfriend Carrie.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Anais and Jamie form an unlikely partnership when they're accused of a crime they didn't commit.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole has decided it's time to throw out the family's refrigerator. This doesn't go down too well with Richard, as it's his favorite possession. When it turns out the refrigerator is also a magical storage facility for childhood emotions and memories, it makes sense that Richard is having a hard time letting go.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans learn about cartoon-style violence while trying to get back a bowl of nuts from a Sqirrel.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin makes the other Titans sell their treasured mementos from past episodes.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg is building stress up, to the point where he directs his built-up rage onto the other Titans. To help calm him down, Starfire takes Cyborg into her own, little secret garden.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans go to watch Lebron James at the Wacka Doodles Amusement Park, but find no one is there.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: It's Valentine's Day and Cyborg goes to great lengths to make it special for his girlfriend Jinx.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy makes lots of money due to a pyramid scheme and convinces the other Titans to join in.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Beast Boy's St. Patrick's Day Luck, and it's Bad
programme.desc: Beast Boy pinches the other Titans on St. Patty's Day, making them pinch him back, resulting in them all getting curse with bad luck.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Teen Titans Go Easter Holiday Classic
programme.desc: The Easter Bunny is missing, and it's up to the Titans to find him before Easter is ruined.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Batman v Teen Titans: Dark Injustice
programme.desc: The Titans try to outprank each other on April Fools' Day.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren gets driven out of his mind yet again as his anxiously awaited cousin Svën forms a stupidity bond with Stimpy through magic Nose Goblins, Kitty Litter, and the "Don't Whiz on the Electric Fence" board game.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy visit a haunted mansion where a depressive ghost fruitlessly tries to scare them, but the clueless duo barely notice his antic... not even the chainsaw!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy, starving, exploit the benefits of living as babies. Then, George Liquor American takes us the Dog Show, where Ren & Stimpy are pitted against some tough competition. But only George can handle the judge's harsh scrutiny.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy squeezes, and a stench is born. Ren thinks it's just a stinky fantasy, but when Stinky floats away, Stimpy abandons his friend to search through rain and snow and dark of night. It'll take a Christmas miracle for Stinky to return to his warm, snugly home!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy want the easy life of living in a zoo. They disguise themselves as monkeys and try to hang in there.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys photocopy a dollar bill and try to use the copies to buy stuff.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys steal Mr. Anderson's steamroller, with predictable results.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: As a class assignment for the weekend, the boys attempt to solve the mystery, but fall asleep before the arrival of the "Morning Wood Fairy".

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys must give oral reports on U.S. history, or face flunking ninth grade; Beavis tells about last week's dinner at Burger World; Butt-head reports on the time he kicked Beavis in the 'nads.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head try to score with a vice cop who is working undercover as a prostitute.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Village People (Ophelia & Shawn) vs. Falcons (Jenny & Brian)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Fabulous T-Birds (Mike & Erin) vs. Pickle Heads (Tracy & John)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Santa Claus Forgets Christmas will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast becomes suspicious that Ross has put something in Doug and Vanessa's food to keep them small, as the two celebrate their 5th anniversary on the show.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rambo's Armpits will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast has to test their knowledge of different smells.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Where No Man Has Gone Before will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ted decides whether or not to tell Chris about his being turned down for a part in a Hollywood movie.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Hulk Hogan Tidies His Room will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
In order to get the cast to clean the set, Ross makes the cleaning part of the sketches.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
E.T. Phones Home and Gets a $1,000,000 Phone Bill will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
An alien ship is approaching Earth and causes the show's transmission satellites to malfunction. The Aliens have come in search of Green Slime.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Jane Fonda Overworked will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Kevin is having trouble writing this episode as it is being filmed. In the sketches, the cast makes fun of exercise programs. Ted, as the Fitness Genie, makes Rekha's fitness program impossible.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Batman's Hairdryer Fries Robin will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The studio's cameras keep breaking because of Ted's ugliness.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Clean Falls Into a Tar Pit will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross makes the cast wear sailor suits to prepare for a visit from the Senate's Committee to Clean Television. In the end, the inspector only wants to be sure the camera lenses are clean.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turkeys will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast wants to take martial arts lessons, but have to teach themselves because the show's budget can't afford an instructor.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
It's a monstrous edition of the world's toughest reality sports show in town as product mascots, captained by Pissin' Boots, the mascot of Cat-Scat Kitty Litter challenges real monsters, captained by Clamula, whose reeking breath kills people for miles around as we celebrate All Hallow's Eve.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Intestinal Fortitude; Dry Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The battle would be settled between the aerospace workers and the fast food employees in MXC's Arctic Edition.
Games include: Sno' Man's Land; Frozen Wallbangers; Sperm Wheelers; Frigid Slope of Icy Death

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Republicans vs. Democrats vs. Third Party
programme.desc:
MXC presents their first ever three-way... battle in honor of the Presidential electoral college entitled "Squeeze Out The Vote 2004" where it's the Republicans against the Democrats against the Third Party. Forget the backstabbing and name-calling because this time, the mud-slinging's for real!
Games include: Rock the Moat; Dark Horse Race; Pork the Barrel; The Rotating Surfboard of Political Suicide; Mudslingers; Log Jam

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The people who can't hold a job are facing off against the tree-huggers who want to save the earth when the unemployed challenge the environmentalists in this MXC battle.
Games include: Great Holes of Glory; Finger It; Dope on a Rope; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; Pole Riders

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Snack Food Industry vs. Print Media
programme.desc:
If you're hungry for action, this episode of MXC is just for you. Members of the snack food industry challenge their arch rivals from the print media in a winner-takes-all battle for bragging rights.. .and a cold soda or two. Also, the Captain introduces everyone to his new book, "Well, You're Wrong!".
Games include: Slippery Slope of Slanted Death; Door Jam; Circle Jerkers; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Baby product makers challenge the people who track down UFOs and ghosts on this edition of MXC in a war to settle their score, and nap time afterward for everyone.
Games include: Mud Butlers; Yankin' It; Flailing Wall; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Oral drillers take on spelunkers on this MXC affair when the dentists take on explorers. Also, Kenny demonstrates some of his new inventions... in which Vic needs some teamsters to help.
Games include: The Dash to Death; Hand Job; Wall Bangers; Sinkers & Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
MXC throws open their games to kids for our first "Children's Special" as the country children take on the city children and see where the best kids come from. Also, Vic and Kenny test out Vic's new invention... a career-choosing device for toddlers.
Games include: Escape From Foster Care; Lil' Sinkers and Floaters; Irritable Bowl Training; Rotating Surfboard of Death Jr.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Another family dinner at the Morgendorffer household.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Upchuck impresses Kevin and Brittany with a card trick.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The Morgendorffer family prepares for some sibling rivalry between Helen and her sister Rita.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria, Jodie and Upchuck are in the running for a top scholarship prize. Unfortunatley, this competition makes Jane feel left out(see "See Jane Run," Episode #2.11), and to make matters worse, she finds that the company that sponsors the scholarship has a record of discriminatory hiring practices. Will the Lawndale brainiac compromise her principles and learn how to be a kiss-up?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Tom enjoy a not-so-quiet evening at home.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria becomes obsessed with a big carton. Who wouldn't?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn arrive at their new school. Quinn makes friends easily. Our Daria does not. We meet Daria's teachers, her family, and her new best friend, Jane.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Grateful for her help in art class, dimwitted cheerleader Brittany Taylor invites Daria to her popular-crowd party in a gated community. Though uninterested, Daria realizes that attending the party would be a perfect opportunity to humiliate Quinn. She takes Jane along for kicks...hopefully.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Having just missed catching Baron Greenback in Tokyo, Dangermouse gets hold of the villain's digital hideout locater (disguised as a book). Using this device, DM and Penfold take a tour of the Baron's various hideouts hidden all around the world.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has persuaded Count Duckula to visit London. Desperate for a TV show of his own, Duckula infects the entire government with his own showbiz sickness so they will agree to his every demand. After capturing Danger Mouse and Penfold, the evil vampire duck subjects them to the absolute worst comedy variety show ever to grace a theater stage. Despite of this, Duckula somehow managed to get his own cartoon series before the decade was over.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A Demon from the Fourth Dimension appears on Danger Mouse's doorstep to challenge the one-eyed white wonder to a duel. Before they can duke it out on the Demon's home turf however, the world's greatest secret agent is sidetracked or rather suckered into his own TV spectacular: 'This Was Your Life'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Proffesor Von Squakenkluck has invented a time machine and accidentally sends Penfold back 150 Million Years into the past in place of a dinosaur egg. Danger Mouse follows in the Mark 12, after changing places with a Diplodocus.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: DM and Penfold have just retrieved a stolen secret fuel sample from Field Marchall Rommole. On their way back, the Mark 5 gets hit by an electric storm, Penfold is struck dumb and the two of them are shot into a distant future where Britain cats are the dominant species. Worse still, a distant relative of Baron Greenback rules the cats under the name of 'Big Leo'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Four Heads Are Better than Two
programme.desc: Colonel K has had his people create robot duplicates of Dangermouse and Penfold, so he can team the actual DM with robot Penfold and vice versa, in order to be able to stop more crime at the same time. The real Dangermouse and the fake Penfold are send to meet master of disguise Agent 57 at the Pinball Palace, where arch villain Baron Greenback is hatching a scheme to hypnotize the world by way of pinball machines.
